findings.description: Destructive UPDATE bundled in seed file — Part 4 unconditionally hides ALL real approved brands (`UPDATE brands SET status = 'hidden' WHERE is_demo = false AND status = 'approved'`). If someone runs this seed file on a production database expecting only additive demo data insertion, all real brands silently become hidden. This should either be separated into its own clearly-named file (e.g., `hide-real-brands.sql`) or at minimum have a prominent warning comment block before the statement.

Demo badge uses raw hex colors (bg-[#EDE8F5], text-[#6B47B8]) not documented in DESIGN.md. While intentionally chosen to be purple/lavender (per design input), these colors should be added to DESIGN.md as semantic tokens (e.g., --demo-badge-bg, --demo-badge-text) to prevent palette drift. The Badges section in DESIGN.md documents Verified and Category but not Demo.

skipped.reason: Test naming uses function-signature framing (brandToDomain -- isDemo) but this is the existing convention for pure mapper unit tests in this file, and consistent with the isVerified test block above.
eslint-disable for @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any is intentional — mapper tests pass minimal objects via `as any` to avoid constructing full Brand types. Follows existing pattern.

Silent no-op when tag slugs don't exist in taxonomy_tags is standard seed file behavior. All referenced tag slugs (food, sustainability, handmade, clothing, beauty, home) verified to exist in migrations/00008 and seed.sql.
